---
title: MODULE TITLE
teaching: 40        # minutes of teaching
exercises: 40       # minutes of exercises
---

::: callout-outcomes
## 💡 Learning Outcomes

- Outcome 1 (what learners will be able to do)  
- Outcome 2  
- Outcome 3  
:::

---

::: callout-questions
## ❓ Questions

1. Key question learners should consider  
2. Another guiding question  
3. A further discussion/reflection question  
:::

## Structure & Agenda

1. Topic 1 (~time)  
2. Topic 2 (~time)  
3. Topic 3 (~time)  
4. Topic 4 (~time)  

> 🔧 Activities spaced throughout the session  

---

# Section Heading (Topic 1)

![](fig/example.png){fig-align="center" width=500px}

## Subheading (e.g., Concept Intro)

Brief explanation text goes here.

---

### Another Subheading (e.g., Why this matters)

- Key point 1  
- Key point 2  
- Key point 3  

---

### Example Table

| Column A   | Column B   | Column C   |
|------------|------------|------------|
| Item 1     | Detail     | Note       |
| Item 2     | Detail     | Note       |

---

::: callout-task
#### Task N: Title of Task (e.g., Reflection / Hands-on Activity)

::: panel-tabset
##### Question
❓ Guiding task question here.  

##### Follow-up
- Step 1 / discussion point  
- Step 2  
- Step 3  

##### Answers (optional)
Provide expected answer(s) or hints here.  
:::
:::

---

# Section Heading (Topic 2)

## Subheading

Explanatory text, diagrams, or code snippets.  

```{r}
# Example R code

# Create a simple numeric vector
numbers <- c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)

# Compute the sum
total <- sum(numbers)

# Print the result
print(paste("The sum is:", total))
```

```{python}
# Example Python code

# Create a simple list of numbers
numbers = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]

# Compute the sum
total = sum(numbers)

# Print the result
print("The sum is:", total)
```

# Further Information

::: callout-keypoints

## 📚 Keypoints

* Somekeypoint

:::

::: callout-hints

## 🔦 Hints

* some hint
:::

## Module Summary

short summary paragraph about the module

## Additional Learning

links to specific external resources. i.e. 
The concepts in this module connect directly to practical data handling and exploration in Python.

| Submodule                | Python Connection                                                   | Why It Matters                                                               |
|--------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Visualisations for Insights | Create bar, line, scatter, and pie charts using matplotlib or seaborn | Demonstrates how to transform numerical summaries into clear visuals.        |
